alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services with two promotions, leading global commercial operations transformation programs and scaling technical and commercial initiatives
alignments.job_requirement: 5+ years of experience accountable for technical project / program delivery within the domain of Data and AI

alignments.candidate_evidence: Google commercial operations leader serving as technical lead across multiple business units, orchestrating cross-functional teams, and managing complex stakeholder relationships at scale
alignments.job_requirement: Experience in a customer-facing pre-sales, technical architecture, customer success, or consulting role

alignments.candidate_evidence: Directed transformation programs, managed cross-functional team coordination, and owned execution of complex initiatives at Google; demonstrated stakeholder and resource orchestration
alignments.job_requirement: Technical program, or project management including account, stakeholder and resource management accountability

alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight-year tenure managing enterprise-scale programs with C-suite and senior stakeholder engagement across Google Devices and Services
alignments.job_requirement: Experience resolving complex and important escalation with senior customer executives

alignments.candidate_evidence: Google transformation program ownership included requirements definition, roadmap design, UAT leadership, and complex program rollout
alignments.job_requirement: Conduct open-ended discovery workshops, create strategic roadmaps, conducting business analysis and managing delivery of complex programmes/projects

alignments.candidate_evidence: Works with SQL (proficient in joins, GROUP BY, subqueries, window functions), BigQuery, Python (basic, actively learning), and designs specifications for LLM coding agents; no production Spark, distributed data platform, or data-engineering background stated
alignments.job_requirement: Understanding of solution architecture related distributed data systems; become the post-sale technical lead across all Databricks products

gaps.gap: Advanced Python and production Spark engineering are core technical requirements; candidate has only basic Python and no Spark or distributed-systems production experience
gaps.mitigation: Candidate is actively learning Python on the pandas/data-analysis track. Databricks could invest in structured Spark and distributed-systems training (typically 3–6 months) if program-delivery, stakeholder-management, and business-value-attribution strengths outweigh the gap. Consider pairing with embedded Spark/platform specialists early in the role.

gaps.gap: No stated experience with Databricks platform, data lakehouse architecture, or modern data-stack tools (Delta Lake, MLflow) beyond basic SQL and BigQuery
gaps.mitigation: Standard onboarding for a new platform. Candidate's SQL querying, data-analysis, and agentic-tool ownership provide a foundation, but lakehouse vision and platform-specific architecture require product training.

gaps.gap: No pre-sales, technical solutions architecture, or customer success background; commercial ops differs from field-facing technical account management
gaps.mitigation: Candidate's internal stakeholder orchestration and transformation leadership transfer partially. Pair with experienced Solutions Architects early; shadow pre-sales engagements and customer discovery sessions to learn customer-engagement and technical-positioning patterns.

gaps.gap: No stated track record of customer quota attainment or overachievement against commercial goals
gaps.mitigation: Candidate managed territory budgets in Salesforce and drove adoption/usage initiatives; these indirect commercial outcomes are relevant but not explicit quota achievement. Clarify customer-expansion metrics and MRR growth from prior role.

gaps.gap: Limited consulting and case-team background; no evidence of leading external discovery workshops or writing customer-facing architecture proposals
gaps.mitigation: Candidate's program-design and stakeholder-management skills transfer, but formal consulting delivery patterns and customer-proposal writing require role-specific training and initial mentorship.

output.level_rationale: 5+ years required experience in technical program/project delivery, post-sales technical account leadership, customer escalation management, and complex delivery orchestration. Reports to DSA Manager (not manager-of-managers). Mid-market or enterprise customer scope at a Series-D+ company. Anchors to L5: established technical-IC ownership of account strategy and delivery, stakeholder management across multiple teams, and pre-sales/post-sales bridge responsibility.
output.summary: Delivery Solutions Architect is a customer-facing technical leadership role requiring 5+ years of technical program/project delivery in data/AI, strong Python and Spark engineering, and pre-sales or solutions architecture experience. The candidate has deep commercial ops, transformation, and stakeholder-management experience at Google but lacks the required advanced Python, production Spark coding, and classic data-platform technical architecture background this DSA role demands as core accountabilities.
uncertainties: Candidate's Python learning velocity and readiness for Spark engineering are unknown; timeline to production-level fluency in Spark is unclear.
Extent of customer-facing work at Google (vs. internal stakeholder management) is not fully specified; confirms field-engagement capability.
Databricks company size and DSA-team maturity are not stated; support infrastructure for onboarding a non-data-engineer into the role is unknown.
